markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games has pushed Grand Theft Auto VI's release to November 19, 2026, following multiple delays from an initial 2025 target, extending the development window and heightening anticipation across gaming culture. This extended timeline positions the market to capture trader sentiment on whether high-profile events—such as geopolitical resolutions or major music releases—will occur in the roughly five months remaining. Strong historical patterns show Rockstar prioritizing polish over rushed launches, while the game's massive cultural footprint makes any pre-release developments particularly salient for assessing momentum shifts in entertainment and pop culture.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrial on the remaining New York rape charge in Harvey Weinstein’s third trial has left prosecutors with until late June to decide on a potential fourth proceeding, while appeals of his 16-year California sentence and additional criminal-sex-act conviction continue. At age 74 and with documented health concerns including wheelchair use during Rikers confinement, Weinstein’s legal team emphasizes time already served and procedural challenges that could limit further incarceration. Traders see these developments, combined with mixed prior verdicts and hung juries on key counts, as tilting strongly toward no additional prison time, with only slim implied odds attached to multi-year enhancements from unresolved cases.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 Stanley Cup Final between the Carolina Hurricanes and Vegas Golden Knights has shaped trader consensus, with the Hurricanes holding a slight edge due to their dominant regular-season performance as Metropolitan Division champions with 113 points and a 12-1 playoff record, including a five-game Eastern Conference Finals win over the Montreal Canadiens. The Golden Knights counter with strong momentum from a third finals appearance in nine franchise seasons, highlighted by a Western Conference Finals sweep of the Colorado Avalanche and consistent depth from players like Jack Eichel. Series odds reflect this closely contested matchup between two possession-oriented teams, with Carolina favored at home for Games 1 and 2 before the series shifts to Las Vegas.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The San Antonio Spurs enter the 2026 NBA Finals as clear favorites in trader consensus, reflecting their league-best 62-20 regular-season mark and efficient playoff run that included a seven-game Western Conference Finals victory over the defending champion Oklahoma City Thunder. Victor Wembanyama anchors a balanced roster with strong defensive versatility and scoring depth, while the team’s late-season surge and overall roster construction position them favorably against the New York Knicks. The Knicks, at 53-29, secured their first Finals appearance since 1999 by navigating a competitive Eastern Conference path behind Jalen Brunson’s postseason production and an 11-game winning streak heading into the series. Their underdog positioning accounts for the tougher conference journey and solid home-court advantages, though the Spurs’ superior record and momentum create the primary separation reflected in current implied probabilities.

This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France lead the tightly bunched field for the 2026 FIFA World Cup title as the June 11 kickoff approaches, reflecting their strong recent international form, deep attacking options, and favorable group-stage positioning. England sits close behind on the strength of its balanced squad and consistent results in qualifying and friendlies, while Portugal and Argentina maintain realistic paths despite roster questions. South American contenders face added pressure from key injuries, including Neymar’s calf issue for Brazil and other absences that could limit depth. The competitive spread stems from the expanded 48-team format, even distribution of top sides across the bracket, and historical precedent for upsets in knockout stages, leaving multiple nations with viable routes to the final at MetLife Stadium.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Traders price the low probability of removal through 2026 based on the absence of elite dissent or procedural mechanisms for early transition, consistent with Xi's pattern of extending authority beyond prior norms.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom holds the strongest trader consensus at 26.4% in the wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market, reflecting his term-limited California governorship, national fundraising reach, and sustained visibility among party donors and officials. This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom holds the strongest trader consensus at 26.4% in the wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market, reflecting his term-limited California governorship, national fundraising reach, and sustained visibility among party donors and officials. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez follows at 9.8% on progressive base mobilization, while Kamala Harris at 7.8% retains name recognition despite the 2024 outcome. Senators Jon Ossoff and Mark Kelly, along with governors Josh Shapiro and Andy Beshear, trail on narrower regional or policy profiles. With no formal declarations yet, the fragmented field leaves room for consolidation through 2026 midterm results, early-state organizing, and Senate confirmation dynamics. Key upcoming catalysts include candidate positioning in battleground regions and primary calendar positioning that could shift implied probabilities.
